evidence_quotes.quote: should not have its access revoked by its publisher as if it were a service on a subscription model

paragraphs.text: The Act contains a good amount of consumer protections that I believe are valuable not only to the EU, but worldwide. I would be thankful if you considered adding the decision that digital media that was previously bought as a *product* (or which had other monetization, like microtransactions, where it is considered a continuous product), be it video games, movies, or any other kind of media, should not have its access revoked by its publisher as if it were a service on a subscription model. A recent situation like this was the video game The Crew (2014), which despite being sold as a product with no expectation of an end of support, had its servers shut down and it has become completely unplayable, even to those who legitimately bought it as a product they expected to have access to indefinitely. I believe the Act could be helpful in providing more protections to consumers regarding this issue and pushing forward the industry to become more clear and fair.
